sourceMovie.title: Kagemusha
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Kagemusha works as a action movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The action and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Rurouni Kenshin Part I: Origins
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Kagemusha clicked for you, Rurouni Kenshin Part I: Origins (2012) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and history, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Rurouni Kenshin: The Final

faq.question: What should I watch after Kagemusha?
faq.answer: Try Rurouni Kenshin Part I: Origins, Rurouni Kenshin: The Final, 300—each shares DNA with Kagemusha while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Kagemusha?
faq.answer: Kagemusha (1980) is best described as Action, Drama, History, War. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Kagemusha worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.8/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
